pending.body: Enforce domain validation during deserialization in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:158-170`. The custom `Deserialize` impl only runs `validate_classification_fields`, so malformed persisted values like an empty `finding_summary`, empty `proposed_title`, or `confidence: 1.5` still deserialize into `FindingClassification` and are only rejected if every caller separately invokes `validate_classification` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:306-378`). For a type whose stated purpose is JSON persistence, that leaves the domain invariant unenforced at the read boundary.

Make `FindingClassification` the live review/final_review payload shape, or add an explicit adapter in the same change. The new enum is defined in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:49-77`, but the active final-review contract still emits `FinalReviewProposal { body, rationale, mapped_to_bead_id }` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:136-160`, and the engine still only distinguishes `mapped_to_bead_id.is_some()` versus a regular amendment in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2702-2714` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2871-2882`. As written, no stage can actually produce structured `fix-now` or `propose-new-bead` findings from this new model.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 8 -> Plan and Implement
